long_description: Cockpit pressurization is lost in flight, increasing hypoxia risk and reducing safe altitude margin.
pilot_actions: Don oxygen immediately, initiate emergency descent as required, limit mission continuation, and recover at a breathable altitude.

long_description: In-flight ECS cooling shortfall causes avionics bay overheating, increasing the risk of computer resets or mission system shutdowns.
pilot_actions: Reduce nonessential avionics load, monitor overheat indications, avoid prolonged high-heat operating modes, and recover before cascading avionics failures occur.

long_description: Loss of engine bleed-air support in flight degrades cockpit conditioning and ECS performance, reducing pressurization margin and thermal control.
pilot_actions: Monitor pressurization and temperature trend, descend if margins deteriorate, reduce mission duration, and recover before additional ECS faults develop.

long_description: Cockpit temperature control malfunctions in flight, causing persistent overheating or overcooling that degrades pilot endurance and equipment comfort margins.
pilot_actions: Adjust alternate conditioning settings if available, reduce task loading, and recover if cockpit conditions begin to affect crew performance.

long_description: Defogging or canopy airflow performance degrades in flight, allowing misting or visibility reduction during humidity or temperature transients.
pilot_actions: Use alternate anti-fog settings if available, avoid unnecessary head-down tasking, and recover before visibility degrades to an unsafe level.
